mechanistic_hypotheses.hypothesis: BRCA2 modulates pathway response under varying exposure burden.
mechanistic_hypotheses.supporting_evidence: Curated integration of association, pathway, and epidemiologic evidence supports this relationship.

molecular_function: Pathway-level interaction under variable exposure conditions
name: BRCA2 DNA Repair Associated

regulatory_notes.enhancer_associations: BRCA2 has enhancer associations in inflammatory and disease-linked contexts.
regulatory_notes.methylation_sensitivity: BRCA2 expression may shift with methylation dynamics and exposure burden.
regulatory_notes.promoter_activity: BRCA2 promoter activity is context-sensitive across curated tissue settings.

summary: BRCA2 is modeled as a disease-relevant locus that links inherited architecture, pathway activity, and environmental sensitivity in GENARCH educational outputs.
